messages.content: I feel for you. I don’t have that problem as I haven’t lived in a house with a garden for years. Now it’s just pots on the window sills, all with different plants. But I remember the garden when everything was green
Gwendolyn here, you have no idea. I may eventually move away, or at the very least, get a different neighbor. If you don’t have a yard, then your plants live only where you want them to.<|im_end
The grass may be greener, but it’s not in your yard.<|im_end|>
And the soil may be more fertile too...<|im_end<|im_end|>
All green and fertile or all brown and dried up?<|im_end|>
Yeah, I have plenty of brown.<|im_end<|im_end|>
Green grass turning brown.<|im_end<|im_end|>
But then you just need to find a new green grass to make your own yard greener.<|im_end|>
He's been out there putting so much energy into it that he's gained 8 pounds.  I'm very proud of him<|im_end|>
You are right It's all about the soil you are planted in.<|im_end<|im_end|>
The one thing I know is no matter where you plant your roots, you will always grow.<|im_end<|im_end|>
Yeah, I have a great yard here but it is always green. I only see brown when I go up to Canada in the winter. This is a small world.<|im_end|>
That is a great idea Moving away is a little extreme but getting a new neighbour might help.<|im_end|>
That is so great to hear. I am glad you are getting enjoyment out of it and that it is bringing you peace.<|im_end|>
I have 2 large gardens and it’s everything from green, to ripe food, to flowers, to new growth and to brown.<|im_end<|im_end|>
Green grass turns brown, and then it’s brown and beautiful again. That is the cycle of life.<|im_end|>
I'm so sorry you have to deal with this Gwendolyn, but at least now you know that the grass really is greener.<|im_end|>
I will leave you now, I need to go into the garden to do some weeding and deadheading.<|im_end<|im_end|>
